notes: Blocker B4. Reads MOTOI-SCHEME-REFERENCE.slat verb records and emits (question, code + brief-explanation) JSONL pairs per :examples entry. Deterministic ordering (verb name).

messages.content: Show me `across-beats` at the novice level.

```motoi
(across-beats 'circle)
```

Request entities arrange in a circle formation; the driver will synchronize to the current beat phase.
Give me an intermediate-level example of `across-beats` in Motoi.

```motoi
(act 'world/spawn (list 'enemy 100 50) 'enemies-spawned)
(across-beats 'diamond)
```

Spawn enemies, then arrange them in a diamond pattern locked to rhythm.
How would an expert use `across-beats`? Show code.

```motoi
(define formation '())
(define (dance-section ctx)
  (let ((formation (if (> (ctx-get 'beat ctx) 32) 'star 'line)))
    (act 'card/emit (list 'formation-change formation) 'await-formation)))
(across-beats formation)
```

Conditional formation selection based on beat counter; descriptor returned and passed to the driver for rhythm-locked execution.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `across-beats`.

```motoi
(across-beats 'line)
```

Make a formation descriptor. Later choreography will use it to place entities.
Show me `across-beats` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(define row (across-beats 'staggered))
(display row)
```

A staggered formation feels more human than a strict grid. Hand-off row to a mover.
Show me `after` at the novice level.

```motoi
(after 2 'check-status)
```

After 2 seconds, transition to check-status state.
Give me an intermediate-level example of `after` in Motoi.

```motoi
(define (cooldown-expired? ctx)
  (let ((last-action (ctx-get 'last-action-time ctx)))
    (if (null? last-action) #t (> (- (time 'now) last-action) 1000))))
(define (do-action ctx)
  (if (cooldown-expired? ctx)
    (act 'world/spawn (list 'effect 0 0) 'action-done)
    (after 0.5 'retry-action ctx)))
```

Check cooldown; if expired, act; otherwise, retry after a short delay.
How would an expert use `after`? Show code.

```motoi
(define (wait-for-response ctx)
  (let ((request-id (ctx-get 'request-id ctx)) (timeout-ms 5000))
    (after (/ timeout-ms 1000) 'timeout (ctx-set 'request-id request-id ctx))))
(define (timeout ctx)
  (escalate 'request-timeout (ctx-get 'request-id ctx)))
```

Schedule a timeout for a remote request; escalate if the timeout fires.
Show me `after-frame` at the novice level.

```motoi
(after-frame (lambda () (console.log 'painted)))
```

Log a message after the frame has painted; confirms the card is visually settled.
Give me an intermediate-level example of `after-frame` in Motoi.

```motoi
(after-frame (lambda () (let ((h (card-rows))) (set! my-height h))))
```

Measure the card's rendered height after layout and paint; cache it in a mutable variable.
How would an expert use `after-frame`? Show code.

```motoi
(define (trigger-animation ctx)
  (after-frame (lambda () (card/emit (list ':animation 'fade-in))))
  (next 'animating ctx))
```

Schedule an animation emission after the card's DOM is settled; transition to animating state immediately.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `after-frame`.

```motoi
(after-frame
  (lambda ()
    (display "paint settled")))
```

Runs once, after the browser has actually painted the frame you just built.
Show me `after-frame` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(begin-frame 'main)
(grid-cell-set! 3 3 1)
(end-frame 'main)
(after-frame
  (lambda ()
    (display (grid-live-count))))
```

Paint a cell, then measure the live count only after paint has finished.
Show me `ai/align` at the novice level.

```motoi
(ai/align 'fish-1 'school 50)
```

Steer fish-1 toward the average heading of all entities tagged 'school within radius 50.
Give me an intermediate-level example of `ai/align` in Motoi.

```motoi
(define (update-flock ctx)
  (for-each (lambda (id) (ai/align id 'boid 100 2.0)) (ctx-get 'boid-ids ctx))
  (next 'flock-moving ctx))
```

Apply alignment to all boid IDs in context, with a force cap of 2.0; then advance the state.
How would an expert use `ai/align`? Show code.

```motoi
(define (choreograph-dance ctx)
  (let ((formation (ctx-get 'formation ctx)) (radius (if (eq? formation 'tight) 30 100)))
    (for-each
      (lambda (dancer-id)
        (ai/align dancer-id 'dancer radius 1.5)
        (ai/cohere dancer-id 'dancer radius 1.0)
        (ai/separate dancer-id 'dancer 20 2.0))
      (ctx-get 'dancer-ids ctx))
    (next 'dancing ctx)))
```

Combine alignment, cohesion, and separation for coordinated group choreography; radius and force scale with formation tightness.
Show me `ai/arrive` at the novice level.

```motoi
(define my-entity 'my-entity)
(ai/arrive my-entity 100 200)
```

Entity steers toward point (100, 200) with default slow-radius and force.
Give me an intermediate-level example of `ai/arrive` in Motoi.

```motoi
(define my-entity 'my-entity)
(let ((target-x 150) (target-y 75))
  (ai/arrive my-entity target-x target-y 30))
```

Provide explicit slow-radius (30 pixels); entity decelerates smoothly in that zone.
How would an expert use `ai/arrive`? Show code.

```motoi
(define (patrol-waypoint entity waypoint-list waypoint-idx)
  (let ((wp (list-ref waypoint-list waypoint-idx))
        (wx (car wp))
        (wy (cadr wp)))
    (ai/arrive entity wx wy 40 8.0)
    (if (< (entity/distance entity wx wy) 10)
        (+ waypoint-idx 1)
        waypoint-idx)))
```

Cycle through patrol points; arrive at each with custom slow-radius and force, advance when close enough.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `ai/arrive`.

```motoi
(ai/arrive 'hero 100 100)
```

Steer the hero to (100, 100). Slows down as it gets close so it doesn't overshoot.
Show me `ai/arrive` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(on-frame
  (lambda ()
    (ai/arrive 'hero 200 50 30 4)))
```

Arrive with a 30-unit slow radius and a steering force of 4. Feels smooth.
Show me `ai/bb-del!` at the novice level.

```motoi
(define entity-42 'entity-42)
(ai/bb-del! entity-42 'patrol-target)
```

Delete the patrol-target key from entity-42's memory.
Give me an intermediate-level example of `ai/bb-del!` in Motoi.

```motoi
(define entity-42 'entity-42)
(let ((was-set (ai/bb-has? entity-42 'alert-level)))
  (when was-set
    (ai/bb-del! entity-42 'alert-level)))
```

Check before delete; only remove if the key exists.
How would an expert use `ai/bb-del!`? Show code.

```motoi
(define (reset-ai-state entity)
  (for-each (lambda (key)
              (ai/bb-del! entity key))
            '(patrol-target alert-level combat-stance last-seen-pos)))
```

Batch-clear multiple blackboard keys; useful for resetting NPC state after a combat phase ends.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `ai/bb-del!`.

```motoi
(ai/bb-set! 'hero 'goal 'chest)
(ai/bb-del! 'hero 'goal)
```

Store a goal, then forget it. The delete returns #t because the key existed.
Show me `ai/bb-del!` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(when (ai/bb-has? 'hero 'temp)
  (ai/bb-del! 'hero 'temp))
```

Only try to delete a key that's actually there. Defensive tidy-up.
Show me `ai/bb-get` at the novice level.

```motoi
(define my-entity 'my-entity)
(ai/bb-get my-entity 'alert-level)
```

Retrieve the alert-level; if not set, return 'no-key.
Give me an intermediate-level example of `ai/bb-get` in Motoi.

```motoi
(define my-entity 'my-entity)
(let ((target (ai/bb-get my-entity 'patrol-target '())))
  (if (equal? target 'no-key)
      (display "No target")
      (ai/arrive my-entity (car target) (cadr target))))
```

Read the patrol-target; if missing, print a message; otherwise arrive at it.
How would an expert use `ai/bb-get`? Show code.

```motoi
(define (behavior-decide entity)
  (let ((alert (ai/bb-get entity 'alert-level 0))
        (seen-pos (ai/bb-get entity 'last-seen-pos null)))
    (cond ((> alert 3) (ai/bb-set! entity 'state 'flee))
          ((not (null? seen-pos)) (ai/bb-set! entity 'state 'investigate))
          (else (ai/bb-set! entity 'state 'idle)))))
```

Decision tree reads two keys with defaults; branches logic on their values and sets next state.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `ai/bb-get`.

```motoi
(ai/bb-set! 'hero 'goal 'chest)
(display (ai/bb-get 'hero 'goal))
```

Store a goal in the blackboard, then read it back.
Show me `ai/bb-get` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(display (ai/bb-get 'hero 'mood 'neutral))
```

If mood isn't set, fall back to 'neutral. Default protects against missing keys.
Show me `ai/bb-has?` at the novice level.

```motoi
(define my-entity 'my-entity)
(ai/bb-has? my-entity 'alert-level)
```

Return #t if alert-level key is stored, #f otherwise.
Give me an intermediate-level example of `ai/bb-has?` in Motoi.

```motoi
(define entity 'entity)
(if (ai/bb-has? entity 'in-combat)
    (ai/pursue entity (ai/bb-get entity 'target-id))
    (ai/wander entity))
```

Branch on key presence: if in-combat is set, pursue; else wander.
How would an expert use `ai/bb-has?`? Show code.

```motoi
(define (update-patrol entity waypoints idx)
  (if (ai/bb-has? entity 'patrol-active?)
      (let ((next-idx (+ idx 1)))
        (ai/bb-set! entity 'current-wp (list-ref waypoints (modulo next-idx (length waypoints))))
        next-idx)
      idx))
```

Only advance patrol if the patrol-active? flag is set; safe, non-crashing branching.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `ai/bb-has?`.

```motoi
(display (ai/bb-has? 'hero 'goal))
```

Does the hero have a 'goal on the blackboard? True or false.
Show me `ai/bb-has?` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(when (not (ai/bb-has? 'hero 'goal))
  (ai/bb-set! 'hero 'goal 'wander))
```

Only assign a goal if the hero doesn't already have one.
Show me `ai/bb-set!` at the novice level.

```motoi
(define my-entity 'my-entity)
(ai/bb-set! my-entity 'patrol-target (list 100 200))
```

Store a waypoint coordinate under the patrol-target key.
Give me an intermediate-level example of `ai/bb-set!` in Motoi.

```motoi
(define (spot-danger entity threat-id threat-pos)
  (ai/bb-set! entity 'alert-level 3)
  (ai/bb-set! entity 'threat-id threat-id)
  (ai/bb-set! entity 'threat-pos threat-pos))
```

Set multiple keys when an event occurs; subsequent ticks read these to decide behavior.
How would an expert use `ai/bb-set!`? Show code.

```motoi
(define (combat-state entity target-entity)
  (ai/bb-set! entity 'in-combat? #t)
  (ai/bb-set! entity 'target-id (entity/id target-entity))
  (ai/bb-set! entity 'combat-start-time (frame))
  (ai/bb-set! entity 'health-at-combat-start (entity/ref target-entity 'hp))
  (ai/bt-tick combat-tree entity))
```

Initialize a multi-key combat state; use those keys as input to the combat behavior tree.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `ai/bb-set!`.

```motoi
(ai/bb-set! 'hero 'goal 'chest)
```

Write 'goal = chest into the hero's blackboard.
Show me `ai/bb-set!` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(on-frame
  (lambda ()
    (ai/bb-set! 'hero 'last-pos (entity/pos 'hero))))
```

Remember the hero's last position each frame. Handy for undo or trailing effects.
Show me `ai/bt-action` at the novice level.

```motoi
(ai/bt-action 'attack)
```

Create an action node that will call the 'attack handler when ticked.
Give me an intermediate-level example of `ai/bt-action` in Motoi.

```motoi
(ai/bt-sequence
  (ai/bt-condition 'has-target?)
  (ai/bt-action 'pursue)
  (ai/bt-action 'attack))
```

Sequence: check condition, then pursue, then attack (all must succeed).
How would an expert use `ai/bt-action`? Show code.

```motoi
(define combat-tree
  (ai/bt-selector
    (ai/bt-sequence
      (ai/bt-condition 'has-target?)
      (ai/bt-action 'attack))
    (ai/bt-sequence
      (ai/bt-condition 'low-health?)
      (ai/bt-action 'flee))
    (ai/bt-action 'wander)))
```

Selector (OR): try attack if target exists; else try flee if low health; else wander. bt-action nodes are leaves in the tree.
Show me `ai/bt-condition` at the novice level.

```motoi
(ai/bt-condition 'has-target?)
```

Create a condition node that will call the 'has-target? handler when ticked.
Give me an intermediate-level example of `ai/bt-condition` in Motoi.

```motoi
(ai/bt-sequence
  (ai/bt-condition 'has-target?)
  (ai/bt-action 'attack))
```

Only attack if has-target? condition succeeds; both must pass in sequence.
How would an expert use `ai/bt-condition`? Show code.

```motoi
(define patrol-tree
  (ai/bt-selector
    (ai/bt-sequence
      (ai/bt-condition 'has-threat?)
      (ai/bt-action 'flee))
    (ai/bt-sequence
      (ai/bt-condition 'waypoint-reached?)
      (ai/bt-action 'advance-patrol))
    (ai/bt-action 'patrol-move)))
```

Selector runs conditions in order: if has-threat? then flee; else if waypoint-reached? then advance; else patrol-move.
Show me `ai/bt-force` at the novice level.

```motoi
(ai/bt-force 'success (ai/bt-action 'experiment))
```

Always report success, even if the 'experiment action fails internally.
Give me an intermediate-level example of `ai/bt-force` in Motoi.

```motoi
(ai/bt-sequence
  (ai/bt-force 'running (ai/bt-condition 'check-ammo?))
  (ai/bt-action 'reload))
```

Force a check to report 'running so reload happens even if the check would normally succeed.
How would an expert use `ai/bt-force`? Show code.

```motoi
(define (retry-subtree max-attempts subtree)
  (define (attempt n)
    (if (>= n max-attempts)
        (ai/bt-force 'failure subtree)
        (let ((status (ai/bt-tick subtree entity-id)))
          (if (equal? status 'success) status (attempt (+ n 1))))))
  (attempt 0))
```

Wrap subtree with forced failure after N attempts to trigger a fallback in a selector.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `ai/bt-force`.

```motoi
(define always-ok
  (ai/bt-force 'success 'try-attack))
(display always-ok)
```

Force the child to look successful. Useful when you want the parent tree to keep going.
Show me `ai/bt-force` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(define keep-trying
  (ai/bt-force 'running 'search))
(display keep-trying)
```

Pin a slow action in running state so higher-level logic knows it's still busy.
Show me `ai/bt-invert` at the novice level.

```motoi
(define my-enemy-id 'my-enemy-id)
(define tree (ai/bt-invert (ai/bt-condition 'target-seen)))
(ai/bt-tick tree my-enemy-id)
```

The tree succeeds if target-seen fails (no target visible), and fails if target-seen succeeds.
Give me an intermediate-level example of `ai/bt-invert` in Motoi.

```motoi
(define guard-id 'guard-id)
(define patrol-blocked
  (ai/bt-invert (ai/bt-sequence
    (ai/bt-condition 'path-clear)
    (ai/bt-action 'move-to-waypoint))))
(ai/bt-tick patrol-blocked guard-id)
```

Inverts a sequence: succeeds only if the whole sequence does not complete (path is blocked or move fails).
How would an expert use `ai/bt-invert`? Show code.

```motoi
(define soldier-id 'soldier-id)
(define defensive
  (ai/bt-selector
    (ai/bt-invert (ai/bt-condition 'health-critical))
    (ai/bt-sequence
      (ai/bt-action 'take-cover)
      (ai/bt-action 'heal))))
(ai/bt-tick defensive soldier-id)
```

Selector tries inverted health check first; if health is NOT critical, succeeds and skips healing. Otherwise executes cover-and-heal sequence.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `ai/bt-invert`.

```motoi
(define not-hungry (ai/bt-invert 'hungry?))
(display not-hungry)
```

Wrap a check to flip its meaning. Now the node succeeds when the child fails.
Show me `ai/bt-invert` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(define at-rest
  (ai/bt-invert (ai/bt-condition 'moving?)))
(display at-rest)
```

Build 'not-moving' from a 'moving?' check without writing a new condition.
Show me `ai/bt-parallel` at the novice level.

```motoi
(define scout-id 'scout-id)
(define three-way
  (ai/bt-parallel 2
    (ai/bt-condition 'see-target)
    (ai/bt-condition 'hear-target)
    (ai/bt-condition 'smell-target)))
(ai/bt-tick three-way scout-id)
```

Succeeds if at least 2 of the 3 senses detect the target.
Give me an intermediate-level example of `ai/bt-parallel` in Motoi.

```motoi
(define robot-id 'robot-id)
(define consensus-move
  (ai/bt-parallel 2
    (ai/bt-action 'move-north)
    (ai/bt-action 'move-forward)
    (ai/bt-action 'navigate-via-gps)))
(ai/bt-tick consensus-move robot-id)
```

Attempts three movement actions; succeeds if at least 2 complete. Models robustness when only some navigation systems may be working.
How would an expert use `ai/bt-parallel`? Show code.

```motoi
(define explorer-id 'explorer-id)
(define robust-mission
  (ai/bt-sequence
    (ai/bt-parallel 1  ; at least one locator must work
      (ai/bt-action 'ping-beacon)
      (ai/bt-action 'check-gps)
      (ai/bt-action 'visual-landmark))
    (ai/bt-parallel 2  ; must have consensus on safety
      (ai/bt-condition 'no-obstacles)
      (ai/bt-condition 'no-radiation)
      (ai/bt-condition 'no-hostiles))))
(ai/bt-tick robust-mission explorer-id)
```

Sequence enforces ordering: first localization (quorum 1 of 3), then safety consensus (quorum 2 of 3). Models multi-stage mission with layered redundancy.
Write a novice Motoi example that uses `ai/bt-parallel`.

```motoi
(define both
  (ai/bt-parallel 2 'walk 'chew-gum))
(display both)
```

A quorum of 2 out of 2. Both children must succeed for the parallel to succeed.
Show me `ai/bt-parallel` at the intermediate level.

```motoi
(define any-two
  (ai/bt-parallel 2 'see 'hear 'smell))
(display any-two)
```

Three senses run in parallel. As long as any two report success, the parallel wins.
Show me `ai/bt-selector` at the novice level.

```motoi
(define scout-id 'scout-id)
(define fallback
  (ai/bt-selector
    (ai/bt-condition 'target-visible)
    (ai/bt-condition 'target-nearby)
    (ai/bt-condition 'patrol-mode)))
(ai/bt-tick fallback scout-id)
```

Succeeds as soon as one condition is true: visible > nearby > patrol. Evaluates left-to-right, stopping at first true.
Give me an intermediate-level example of `ai/bt-selector` in Motoi.

```motoi
(define guard-id 'guard-id)
(define attack-or-retreat
  (ai/bt-selector
    (ai/bt-sequence
      (ai/bt-condition 'see-target)
      (ai/bt-action 'shoot))
    (ai/bt-sequence
      (ai/bt-condition 'health-low)
      (ai/bt-action 'flee))))
(ai/bt-tick attack-or-retreat guard-id)
```

Try (see & shoot); if that fails, try (low health & flee). Returns on first sequence that succeeds.
How would an expert use `ai/bt-selector`? Show code.

```motoi
(define soldier-id 'soldier-id)
(define evasion-priority
  (ai/bt-selector
    (ai/bt-sequence
      (ai/bt-condition 'missile-incoming)
      (ai/bt-action 'dodge))
    (ai/bt-sequence
      (ai/bt-condition 'enemy-close)
      (ai/bt-action 'melee-counter))
    (ai/bt-sequence
      (ai/bt-condition 'enemy-visible)
      (ai/bt-action 'strafe-and-shoot))
    (ai/bt-action 'patrol)))
(ai/bt-tick evasion-priority soldier-id)
```

Urgent threats first (dodge missile), then close-range (melee), then ranged (strafe), finally default (patrol). Selector enforces priority.
Show me `ai/bt-sequence` at the novice level.

```motoi
(define archer-id 'archer-id)
(define simple-attack
  (ai/bt-sequence
    (ai/bt-condition 'see-target)
    (ai/bt-action 'aim)
    (ai/bt-action 'fire)))
(ai/bt-tick simple-attack archer-id)
```

Tick in order: check sight, aim, fire. If sight fails, stop immediately—never aim or fire.
Give me an intermediate-level example of `ai/bt-sequence` in Motoi.

```motoi
(define guard-id 'guard-id)
(define patrol-and-investigate
  (ai/bt-selector
    (ai/bt-sequence
      (ai/bt-condition 'sound-detected)
      (ai/bt-action 'move-to-sound)
      (ai/bt-action 'look-around))
    (ai/bt-action 'patrol)))
(ai/bt-tick patrol-and-investigate guard-id)
```

Selector offers two strategies: sequence (sound → move → look) or default patrol. Sequence enforces prerequisite order: only move if sound was detected.
How would an expert use `ai/bt-sequence`? Show code.

```motoi
(define operative-id 'operative-id)
(define multi-stage-mission
  (ai/bt-sequence
    (ai/bt-parallel 1
      (ai/bt-action 'check-gps)
      (ai/bt-action 'ask-teammate))
    (ai/bt-action 'navigate-to-waypoint)
    (ai/bt-sequence
      (ai/bt-condition 'area-clear)
      (ai/bt-action 'set-charges)
      (ai/bt-action 'retreat-to-safe-zone))
    (ai/bt-action 'radio-success)))
(ai/bt-tick multi-stage-mission operative-id)
```

Sequence enforces stages: locate (with redundancy via parallel), move, clear-and-plant (conditional sequence), report success. Failure at any stage stops the mission.
Show me `ai/bt-tick` at the novice level.

```motoi
(define my-entity-id 'my-entity-id)
(define simple-tree (ai/bt-action 'patrol))
(define status (ai/bt-tick simple-tree my-entity-id))
(display status)
```

Simplest case: a single action node. ai/bt-tick calls the registered 'patrol' action and returns its result.
Give me an intermediate-level example of `ai/bt-tick` in Motoi.

```motoi
(define goblin-id 'goblin-id)
(define tree
  (ai/bt-sequence
    (ai/bt-condition 'target-seen)
    (ai/bt-action 'move-to-target)
    (ai/bt-action 'shoot)))

(define (on-tick)
  (let ((outcome (ai/bt-tick tree goblin-id)))
    (cond ((equal? outcome 'success) (display "Attack succeeded!"))
          ((equal? outcome 'failure) (display "Attack failed")))))

(on-tick)
```

Each call to ai/bt-tick executes the tree once: check sight, move, shoot. Return status tells you if the attack completed.
How would an expert use `ai/bt-tick`? Show code.

```motoi
(define all-agents '())
(define combat-tree
  (ai/bt-selector
    (ai/bt-sequence
      (ai/bt-condition 'see-enemy)
      (ai/bt-action 'strafe-and-shoot))
    (ai/bt-sequence
      (ai/bt-condition 'hear-enemy)
      (ai/bt-action 'move-toward-sound))
    (ai/bt-action 'patrol)))

(define (game-loop)
  (for-each (lambda (agent)
    (let ((result (ai/bt-tick combat-tree agent)))
      (when (equal? result 'running) (display "Action ongoing\n"))))
    all-agents))

(game-loop)
```

Typical game loop: tick the behavior tree once per agent per frame. The tree encodes the entire decision hierarchy (see → strafe, hear → approach, else patrol). Stateful actions (those returning 'running') are resumed each tick.
Show me `ai/clear!` at the novice level.

```motoi
(define grid (ai/grid 10 10))
(ai/wall! grid 5 5)
(ai/clear! grid 5 5)
(ai/passable? grid 5 5)
```

Wall cell (5,5), then clear it. Passable? returns #t after clearing.
Give me an intermediate-level example of `ai/clear!` in Motoi.

```motoi
(define arena (ai/grid 16 16))

(define (gate-opens)
  (display "Gate opens!\n")
  (ai/clear! arena 8 8)
  (ai/clear! arena 8 9))

(define path (ai/path arena 1 1 15 15))
(gate-opens)
```

Compute A* path, convert to waypoints, steer along it
How would an expert use `ai/follow-path`? Show code.

```motoi
(define (drop xs n)
  (let loop ((xs xs) (n n))
    (if (or (zero? n) (null? xs)) xs (loop (cdr xs) (- n 1)))))
(define (patrol-route ctx)
  (let* ((checkpoints (ctx-get 'patrol-points ctx))
         (current-checkpoint (ctx-get 'current-checkpoint ctx))
         (route (drop (ctx-get 'patrol-path ctx) current-checkpoint))
         (guard-id (ctx-get 'guard-id ctx)))
    (if (null? route)
      (next 'idle (ctx-set 'current-checkpoint 0 ctx))
      (let ((applied (ai/follow-path guard-id route 2.0 4.0)))
        (if (< (+ (abs (car applied)) (abs (cadr applied))) 0.1)
          (next 'patrol (ctx-set 'current-checkpoint (+ current-checkpoint 1) ctx))
          (next 'patrol ctx))))))
```

```motoi
(define (poly-measure beats-a beats-b)
  ;; Interleave two polyrhythm measures: N-in-M against M-in-N.
  ;; Return the list of beat records in interleaved order.
  (let loop ((a 0) (b 0) (acc '()))
    (cond ((and (>= a beats-a) (>= b beats-b))
           (reverse acc))
          ((>= a beats-a)
           (loop a (+ b 1) (cons (beat/on b beats-b) acc)))
          ((>= b beats-b)
           (loop (+ a 1) b (cons (beat/on a beats-a) acc)))
          ((< (/ a beats-a) (/ b beats-b))
           (loop (+ a 1) b (cons (beat/on a beats-a) acc)))
          (else
           (loop a (+ b 1) (cons (beat/on b beats-b) acc))))))
(length (poly-measure 3 4))
```

Interleaved 3-against-4 polyrhythm. Returns 7 (3+4 records interleaved by fractional position). A classic hemiola pattern.

```motoi
      (if (< i lo) acc (loop (- i 1) (cons i acc))))))
(define (run-game initial-entities)
```

```motoi
(define (game-loop initial-world)
  (let ((gid (big-bang initial-world)))
    (define (tick-step)
      (if (game/running? gid)
        (begin (game/step gid)
               (let ((state (game/state gid)))
                 (card-emit 'frame-done (list 'frame (game/frame gid) 'entities state))
                 (if (>= (game/frame gid) 3600)
                   (game/stop gid)
                   (register-tick 'game-loop-step (lambda () (tick-step))))))
        (card-emit 'game-finished state)))
    (tick-step)
    gid))
(game-loop '())
```

How would an expert use `calc/arc-length-param`? Show code.

```motoi
(define (cubic-bezier p0 p1 p2 p3 t)
  ;; Cubic Bézier curve: B(t) = (1−t)³P₀ + 3(1−t)²tP₁ + 3(1−t)t²P₂ + t³P₃
  (define (lerp a b s) (+ (* a (- 1 s)) (* b s)))
  (define (quad-lerp a b c s)
    (lerp (lerp a b s) (lerp b c s) s))
  (define (cubic-lerp a b c d s)
    (lerp (quad-lerp a b c s) (quad-lerp b c d s) s))
  (cubic-lerp p0 p1 p2 p3 t))
(define (bezier-length p0 p1 p2 p3)
  ;; Length of a cubic Bézier curve from t=0 to t=1
  (calc/arc-length-param
    (lambda (t) (cubic-bezier (car p0) (car p1) (car p2) (car p3) t))
    (lambda (t) (cubic-bezier (cdr p0) (cdr p1) (cdr p2) (cdr p3) t))
    0 1))
(bezier-length '(0 . 0) '(1 . 2) '(3 . 1) '(4 . 0))
;; => ≈ length of the curve
```

```motoi
(define cubic (lambda args (list "cubic" args)))
(define (filter pred xs)
  (let loop ((xs xs) (acc '()))
    (cond ((null? xs) (reverse acc))
          ((pred (car xs)) (loop (cdr xs) (cons (car xs) acc)))
          (else (loop (cdr xs) acc)))))
(define (find-extrema-and-classify f a b)
  ;; Find critical points and classify each as max, min, or saddle
  (let ((cpts (calc/critical-points-1d f a b)))
    (map (lambda (x)
           (let ((extrema (calc/extrema-1d f a b)))
             ;; extrema returns a list of classified points
             ;; Filter those matching x
             (let ((match (filter (lambda (e) (< (abs (- (car e) x)) 0.000001)) extrema)))
               (if (null? match) (list x 'unknown) (car match)))))
         cpts)))
;; Example usage (hypothetical matching with calc/extrema-1d output)
(find-extrema-and-classify cubic -2 2)
;; => ((−1 'maximum) (1 'minimum))
```

```motoi
  (let loop ((xs xs) (acc init))
    (if (null? xs) acc (loop (cdr xs) (f acc (car xs))))))
(define (adaptive-approx f a x-val orders)
  ;; Try successive orders, return first that converges well
  (let loop ((n 1) (prev-val 0))
    (if (> n orders)
      prev-val
      (let* ((poly (calc/taylor f a n))
             (val (fold-left (lambda (acc i)
               (+ acc (* (list-ref poly i) (pow (- x-val a) i))))
               0 (range n))))
        (if (< (abs (- val prev-val)) 0.000001)
          val
          (loop (+ n 1) val))))))
```

```motoi
(define (gradient-descent f a learning-rate steps)
  (let loop ((pos a) (i 0))
    (if (>= i steps)
      pos
      (let ((grad (calc/gradient f pos)))
        (let ((step-vec (map (lambda (g) (* (- learning-rate) g)) grad)))
          (let ((new-pos (map + pos step-vec)))
            (loop new-pos (+ i 1))))))))
;; Or, at each step, use total-differential to estimate new f value before moving:
(define (approx-step f pos lr)
  (let ((grad (calc/gradient f pos)))
    (let ((delta (map (lambda (g) (* (- lr) g)) grad)))
      (let ((est-df (calc/total-differential f pos delta)))
        (list (map + pos delta) est-df)))))
(approx-step (lambda (xy) (+ (* (car xy) (car xy)) (* (cadr xy) (cadr xy))))
  (list 1.0 1.0) 0.01)
```

```motoi
(define (design-tank target-vol height)
  ;; Find profile f such that rotating f about x-axis gives desired volume
  ;; Use bisection to search for the scaling factor
  (let ((base-profile (lambda (x) (* 0.2 (sqrt (+ 1 (* 0.5 x)))))))
    (let loop ((scale-low 0.5) (scale-high 2.0))
      (if (< (- scale-high scale-low) 0.001)
        scale-low
        (let* ((scale-mid (/ (+ scale-low scale-high) 2.0))
               (scaled-f (lambda (x) (* scale-mid (base-profile x))))
               (vol (calc/volume-revolution scaled-f 0 height 'x)))
          (if (< vol target-vol)
            (loop scale-mid scale-high)
            (loop scale-low scale-mid)))))))
(define tank-scale (design-tank 100 5))
(display (list "Tank scale factor:" tank-scale))
```

```motoi
  ;; Return ((element . pct) ... ) for a compound
  (let ((total-mass (chem/molar-mass formula))
        (elem-counts (chem/formula-counts formula)))

        (list elem (/ (* count (chem/atomic-weight elem)) total-mass))))
      elem-counts)))
```

```motoi
(define (titration-analysis titrant-formula titrant-vol-mL analyte-formula analyte-mass-g)
  ;; Assume 1:1 stoichiometry for simplicity
  (let ((titrant-molarity 0.1))
    (let ((titrant-moles (* titrant-molarity (/ titrant-vol-mL 1000.0))))
      (let ((expected-moles (chem/moles analyte-formula analyte-mass-g)))
        (let ((recovery (/ titrant-moles expected-moles)))
          (display (list "Expected moles of" analyte-formula ":" expected-moles))
          (display (list "Titrant moles (0.1 M):" titrant-moles))
          (display (list "Recovery:" recovery)))))))
(titration-analysis "HCl" 20.0 "NaOH" 0.4)
```

How would an expert use `curve/curvature-2d`? Show code.

```motoi
(define (iota n)
  (let loop ((i 0) (acc '()))
    (if (= i n) (reverse acc) (loop (+ i 1) (cons i acc)))))
(define (filter pred xs)
  (let loop ((xs xs) (acc '()))
    (cond ((null? xs) (reverse acc))
          ((pred (car xs)) (loop (cdr xs) (cons (car xs) acc)))
          (else (loop (cdr xs) acc)))))
(define (find-inflections curve t-min t-max n-samples)
  (define t-step (/ (- t-max t-min) n-samples))
  (define ts (map (lambda (i) (+ t-min (* i t-step)))
                  (iota (+ n-samples 1))))
  (filter (lambda (t)
            (let ((kappa (curve/curvature-2d curve t)))
              (not (eq? kappa 'singular))))
          ts))
(find-inflections (curve/catmull-rom (list (list 0 0) (list 1 1) (list 2 0) (list 3 1))) 0 3 100)
```

```motoi
(define (verify-brachistochrone)
  ;; For a #t brachistochrone, the residual of the action functional should be zero
  ;; This is a symbolic check; in practice, numerical residuals measure discretization error
  (let ((L (lambda (q q-dot)
             ;; Effective Lagrangian for brachistochrone (T − V with reparametrization)
             (/ q-dot (sqrt (+ 1 (* q-dot q-dot)))))))
    (define (check-point t q-val q-dot-val q-accel-val)
      (list 't t
            'residual (curve/euler-lagrange-residual L q-val q-dot-val q-accel-val)))
    (map (lambda (t) (check-point t 1 0.5 (- 0.5)))
         (list 0.1 0.2 0.3))))
(verify-brachistochrone)
```

```motoi
(define (arc-length-numeric curve a b n)
  ;; Approximate arc length via Riemann sum of chord lengths
  (let ((dt (/ (- b a) n)))
    (let loop ((i 0) (sum 0))
      (if (>= i n)
        sum
        (let* ((t1 (+ a (* i dt)))
               (t2 (+ t1 dt))
               (p1 (curve/eval curve t1))
               (p2 (curve/eval curve t2))
               (dx (- (car p2) (car p1)))
               (dy (- (cadr p2) (cadr p1)))
               (chord (sqrt (+ (* dx dx) (* dy dy)))))
          (loop (+ i 1) (+ sum chord))))))
(arc-length-numeric (lambda (t) (list (cos t) (sin t))) 0 6.28 100))
```

Compute arc length of a unit circle by summing chord lengths (approximate); actual arc length is 2π ≈ 6.28.

```motoi
(define (trace-fall-path h x0 y0 n-steps dt)
  ;; Integrate the fall line to build a descent trajectory
  (let loop ((x x0) (y y0) (i 0) (path (list (list x0 y0))))
    (if (>= i n-steps)
      path
      (let* ((fall (curve/fall-line h x y))
             (dx (car fall))
             (dy (cadr fall))
             (x-new (+ x (* dt dx)))
             (y-new (+ y (* dt dy))))
        (loop x-new y-new (+ i 1) (cons (list x-new y-new) path))))))
(trace-fall-path (lambda (x y) (- (+ (* x x) (* y y)))) 3 4 50 0.1)
```

```motoi
(define (flow-field-visualization h x-min x-max y-min y-max grid-n)
  ;; For each grid point, trace a short descent path to show the flow field
  (define x-step (/ (- x-max x-min) grid-n))
  (define y-step (/ (- y-max y-min) grid-n))
  (let loop ((i 0) (flows '()))
    (if (>= i (* grid-n grid-n))
      flows
      (let* ((xi (modulo i grid-n))
             (yi (quotient i grid-n))
             (x (+ x-min (* xi x-step)))
             (y (+ y-min (* yi y-step)))
             (path (curve/fall-path h x y 20)))
        (loop (+ i 1) (cons path flows))))))

                          -2 2 -2 2 4)
```

```motoi
(define ctx '())
(define (classify-and-route task-kind ctx)
  (let* ((classification (domain/of task-kind))
         (domain-name (cdr (assoc 'domain classification)))
         (reads-needed (cdr (assoc 'reads classification))))
    (cond ((null? reads-needed)
           (next 'handle-locally (ctx-set 'classification classification ctx)))
          ((equal? domain-name 'web-research)
           (act 'ask/reasoner reads-needed 'fetch-knowledge))
          (else
           (escalate 'unroutable (list 'domain domain-name 'reads reads-needed))))))
(classify-and-route 'data-loss (ctx-set 'task 'data-loss ctx))
```

```motoi
    (/ loop-gain (+ 1 loop-gain))))
```

```motoi
(define (steer-toward-target e tx ty)
  (let* ((pos (entity/pos e))
         (x (car pos))
         (y (cadr pos))
         (dx (- tx x))
         (dy (- ty y))
         (dist (entity/distance e tx ty)))
    (if (> dist 1)
      (entity/accel! e (* 5 (/ dx dist)) (* 5 (/ dy dist))))))
(steer-toward-target 1 100 100)
(world/step)
```

How would an expert use `entity/accel!`? Show code.

```motoi
(define (flock-steer id neighbors target-speed align-weight)
  (let ((sum-accel (list 0 0)))
    (for-each (lambda (nid)
      (let* ((my-pos (entity/pos id))
             (n-pos (entity/pos nid))
             (dx (- (car n-pos) (car my-pos)))
             (dy (- (cadr n-pos) (cadr my-pos))))
        (if (> (+ (* dx dx) (* dy dy)) 0.1)
          (let ((d (sqrt (+ (* dx dx) (* dy dy)))))
            (entity/accel! id
              (* align-weight (/ dx d))
              (* align-weight (/ dy d)))))))
      neighbors))
  (world/step))
```

Apply alignment steering from a flock; each neighbor contributes a weighted push.

```motoi
(define (run-state-machine entity input)
  (let ((st (entity/state entity)))
    (cond ((equal? st 'idle)
           (if (> input 0) (entity/goto! entity 'running)))
          ((equal? st 'running)
           (if (= input 0) (entity/goto! entity 'idle))
           (if (< input 0) (entity/goto! entity 'backing)))
          ((equal? st 'backing)
           (if (= input 0) (entity/goto! entity 'idle))))))
(let ((char (world/spawn 'char 50 50)))
  (entity/goto! char 'idle)
  (run-state-machine char 1)
  (run-state-machine char 0)
  (run-state-machine char -1))
```

```motoi
  (let ((s (entity/state id))
        (target (world/find 'player)))
    (cond ((eq? s 'idle)

             (entity/goto! id 'chase)

          ((eq? s 'chase)

          ((eq? s 'attack)

           (on-tick 10 (lambda (_)
             (entity/goto! id 'chase)))))))
```

```motoi
(define (run-plan steps)
  (let loop ((rest steps) (done '()))
    (cond ((null? rest) (reverse done))
          (else (let ((step (car rest)))
                  (guard (e ((eq? (error-object-type e) 'skip)
                             (loop (cdr rest) done))
                            ((eq? (error-object-type e) 'abort)
                             (escalate 'plan-aborted (reverse done))))
                    (loop (cdr rest) (cons (step) done))))))))

  (lambda () 'first)
  (lambda () (escalate 'skip))
  (lambda () 'third)))
```

```motoi
(define (multi-heap-moves heaps)
  (fold-left
    (lambda (results i)
      (let ((h (list-ref heaps i)))
        (fold-left
          (lambda (acc remove)
            (when (> h remove)
              (let ((new-heaps (map (lambda (j) (if (= j i) (- h remove) (list-ref heaps j)))
                                     (range 0 (length heaps)))))
                (cons new-heaps acc))))
          results
          '(1 2))))
    '()
    (range 0 (length heaps))))
(game/grundy '((3 5 7)) multi-heap-moves)
```

Test whether a position is a second-player win (nim-sum 0) or first-player win (nim-sum non-zero).
How would an expert use `game/nim-sum`? Show code.

```motoi
(define (bitwise-xor a b) (let loop ((a a) (b b) (out 0) (bit 1)) (if (and (= a 0) (= b 0)) out (loop (quotient a 2) (quotient b 2) (+ out (* bit (modulo (+ a b) 2))) (* bit 2)))))
(define (fold-left f init xs)
  (let loop ((xs xs) (acc init))
    (if (null? xs) acc (loop (cdr xs) (f acc (car xs))))))
(define (find-winning-moves heaps)
  (let ((target (game/nim-sum heaps)))
    (fold-left
      (lambda (moves i)
        (let ((h (list-ref heaps i))
              (new-h (bitwise-xor (list-ref heaps i) target)))
          (if (< new-h h)
            (cons (list ':heap i ':remove (- h new-h)) moves)
            moves)))
      '()
      (range 0 (length heaps)))))
(find-winning-moves '(3 5 6))
```

If nim-sum is non-zero, find all moves that reduce it to 0 by XORing each heap with the nim-sum.

```motoi
  (let ((interior (geom/interior-angle-deg n))
        (exterior (- 180 (geom/interior-angle-deg n))))
    (letrec ((walk (lambda (step x y heading path)
                     (if (= step n) (reverse path)
                         (let ((next-x (+ x (* side-length (cos (geom/->radians heading)))))
                               (next-y (+ y (* side-length (sin (geom/->radians heading)))))
                               (next-heading (+ heading exterior)))

                                 (cons (list next-x next-y) path)))))))
```

```motoi
(define cx (lambda args (list "cx" args)))
(define cy (lambda args (list "cy" args)))
(define (fold-left f init xs)
  (let loop ((xs xs) (acc init))
    (if (null? xs) acc (loop (cdr xs) (f acc (car xs))))))
(define (nearest-corner origin corners)
  (let ((ox (car origin)) (oy (cadr origin)))
    (fold-left (lambda (best corner)
      (let ((cx (car corner)) (cy (cadr corner))
            (dist (geom/pythagoras-hypotenuse (- cx ox) (- cy oy))))
        (if (< dist (caddr best)) (list cx cy dist) best)))
      (list (car (car corners)) (cadr (car corners)) 999999)
      (cdr corners))))
(define origin '(0 0))
(define corners '((3 4) (1 1) (5 5)))
(nearest-corner origin corners)
```

```motoi
(define (rosette-vertices n)
  ;; Generate n equally-spaced points on the unit circle by rotating (1,0)
  ;; by k*(2*pi/n) for k = 0..n-1. Classic construction for regular polygons.
  (let loop ((k 0) (acc '()))
    (if (>= k n)
        (reverse acc)
        (let ((theta (/ (* 2 3.141592653589793 k) n)))
          (loop (+ k 1)
                (cons (geom/rotate 1 0 theta) acc))))))
(length (rosette-vertices 8))
```

```motoi
(define (fold-left f init xs)
  (let loop ((xs xs) (acc init))
    (if (null? xs) acc (loop (cdr xs) (f acc (car xs))))))
(define (count-live-neighbors col row)
  (let* ((deltas (list -1 0 1))
         (candidates (fold-left (lambda (acc dx)
                   (fold-left (lambda (a2 dy)
                     (if (and (not (and (= dx 0) (= dy 0)))
                              (grid-cell? (+ col dx) (+ row dy)))
                       (+ a2 1) a2))
                     acc deltas))
                   0 deltas)))
    candidates))
(count-live-neighbors 10 10)
```

```motoi
  (let* ((origin (grid-origin))
```

```motoi
(define (wireworld-rule state nHeads)
  (cond ((= state 0) 0)
        ((= state 1) 2)                  ;; electron head → tail
        ((= state 2) 3)                  ;; tail → conductor
        ((= state 3)                     ;; conductor stays, or becomes head if 1-2 heads neighbor
         (let ((head-count (count-state-in-neighbors 1)))
           (if (and (>= head-count 1) (<= head-count 2)) 1 3)))
        (else 0)))
(define (tick ctx)
  (grid-step-4state wireworld-rule)
  (paint-grid-4state 'surface (list "#000" "#0ff" "#f00" "#0f0"))
  (if (grid-live-count) (next 'tick ctx) (escalate 'simulation-halted null)))
(on-tick (lambda () (tick (make-ctx))))
```

```motoi
(define (find-closest-flower target-x target-y)
  (define (min-by-distance ids best-id best-dist)
    (if (null? ids) best-id
      (let* ((id (car ids))
             (pos (grid/flower-pos id)))
        (if pos
          (let* ((dx (- (car pos) target-x))
                 (dy (- (cdr pos) target-y))
                 (dist (sqrt (+ (* dx dx) (* dy dy)))))
            (if (< dist best-dist)
              (min-by-distance (cdr ids) id dist)
              (min-by-distance (cdr ids) best-id best-dist)))
          (min-by-distance (cdr ids) best-id best-dist)))))
  (let ((ids (grid/flower-list)))
    (if (null? ids) #f
      (min-by-distance (cdr ids) (car ids) 999999))))
```

```motoi
(define (take xs n)
  (let loop ((xs xs) (n n) (acc '()))
    (if (or (zero? n) (null? xs))
        (reverse acc)
        (loop (cdr xs) (- n 1) (cons (car xs) acc)))))
(define (target-priority ctx)
  (let* ((enemies (group/find 'enemy))
         (by-dist (sort enemies (lambda (a b)
           (< (entity/distance a *player-id*)
              (entity/distance b *player-id*)))))
         (nearest-3 (take 3 by-dist)))
    (group/each 'turret (lambda (turret-id)
      (let ((target (car nearest-3)))
        (entity/set! turret-id 'target target)
        (set! nearest-3 (cdr nearest-3)))))
    (next 'target-priority ctx)))
(on-tick (lambda () (target-priority (make-ctx))))
```

```motoi
(define (every pred xs)
  (let loop ((xs xs))
    (cond ((null? xs) #t) ((not (pred (car xs))) #f) (else (loop (cdr xs))))))
(define (some pred xs)
  (let loop ((xs xs))
    (cond ((null? xs) #f) ((pred (car xs)) #t) (else (loop (cdr xs))))))
(define (lyapunov-candidate A)
  (let ((evals (linalg/eigenvalues-3x3 A)))

      ((every (lambda (e) (< e 0)) evals) 'stable)
      ((some (lambda (e) (> e 0)) evals) 'unstable)
      (else 'marginal))))
```

```motoi
(define (delete-duplicates xs)
  (let loop ((xs xs) (acc (quote ())))
    (cond ((null? xs) (reverse acc))
          ((member (car xs) acc) (loop (cdr xs) acc))
          (else (loop (cdr xs) (cons (car xs) acc))))))
(define (diagonalize A)
  (let ((evals (linalg/eigenvalues A))
        (unique-evals (delete-duplicates (linalg/eigenvalues A))))
    (let ((evectors (map (lambda (e) (linalg/eigenvectors A e)) unique-evals)))
      (list 'eigenvalues unique-evals 'eigenvectors evectors))))
(define A (matrix/make 3 3 '(2 1 0 0 3 1 0 0 4)))
```


Orthonormalize columns of a matrix (or column vectors); useful for change of basis.
How would an expert use `linalg/gram-schmidt`? Show code.

```motoi
(define (list->vec xs) (list->vector xs))
(define vec->list 'vec->list)
(define (iota n)
  (let loop ((i 0) (acc '()))
    (if (= i n) (reverse acc) (loop (+ i 1) (cons i acc)))))
(define (stable-qr-gram-schmidt A)
  (define cols (matrix/cols A))
  (define col-vecs (map (lambda (i)
    (list->vec (map (lambda (j) (matrix/ref A j i)) (iota (matrix/rows A)))))
    (iota cols)))
  (let ((Q (linalg/gram-schmidt col-vecs))
        (R (matrix/make cols cols '())))
    (list 'Q (apply matrix/make (matrix/rows A) cols (apply append (map vec->list Q)))
          'R R)))
(define A (matrix/make 3 2 '(1 0 1 1 0 1)))
(stable-qr-gram-schmidt A)
```

```motoi
(define (pow x y) (expt x y))
(define (fold-left f init xs)
  (let loop ((xs xs) (acc init))
    (if (null? xs) acc (loop (cdr xs) (f acc (car xs))))))
(define (fit-polynomial points degree)
  (let* ((n (length points))
         (A-data (fold-left
                  (lambda (acc i)
                    (let* ((x (car (list-ref points i)))
                           (row (fold-left
                                 (lambda (r d) (append r (list (pow x d))))
                                 '()
                                 (range 0 (+ degree 1)))))
                      (append acc row)))
                  '()
                  (range 0 n)))
         (A (matrix/make n (+ degree 1) A-data))
         (b (map cdr points)))
    (linalg/least-squares A b)))
(define data '((1 . 1.1) (2 . 4.0) (3 . 9.2) (4 . 15.9)))
(fit-polynomial data 2)
```

How would an expert use `math/avg`? Show code.

```motoi
(define (take xs n)
  (let loop ((xs xs) (n n) (acc '()))
    (if (or (zero? n) (null? xs))
        (reverse acc)
        (loop (cdr xs) (- n 1) (cons (car xs) acc)))))
(define (adaptive-threshold readings window-size)
  (let loop ((readings readings)
            (window (list))
            (thresholds (list)))
    (if (null? readings)
      (math/avg thresholds)
      (let* ((new-window (cons (car readings) (if (>= (length window) window-size)
                                                   (take window (- window-size 1))
                                                   window)))
             (threshold (math/avg new-window)))
        (loop (cdr readings) new-window (cons threshold thresholds))))))
(adaptive-threshold (list 10 12 11 9 8 15 14 13) 3)
```

Use math/avg in a rolling window to compute adaptive thresholds.

How would an expert use `math/compare`? Show code.

```motoi
(define (filter pred xs)
  (let loop ((xs xs) (acc '()))
    (cond ((null? xs) (reverse acc))
          ((pred (car xs)) (loop (cdr xs) (cons (car xs) acc)))
          (else (loop (cdr xs) acc)))))
(define (order-numbers lst)
  (if (null? (cdr lst))
    lst
    (let ((pivot (car lst))
          (rest (cdr lst)))
      (let ((smaller (filter (lambda (x) (equal? (math/compare x pivot) '<)) rest))
            (larger (filter (lambda (x) (equal? (math/compare x pivot) '>)) rest)))
        (append (order-numbers smaller) (list pivot) (order-numbers larger))))))
```

Partition-based sort using compare as the ordering relation

```motoi
(define (matrix-fixed-point? f seed max-iter)
  ;; Return #t if applying f converges to a matrix that equals its own
  ;; f-image within max-iter steps. Uses matrix/= for termination.
  (let loop ((cur seed) (i 0))
    (if (>= i max-iter) #f
        (let ((next (f cur)))
          (if (matrix/= next cur) #t
              (loop next (+ i 1)))))))
(matrix-fixed-point? (lambda (M) (matrix/* M M)) (matrix/identity 3) 5)
```

Fixed-point detector: does iterating f eventually stabilize? Identity is a fixed point of matrix squaring, so returns #t on iteration 1.

```motoi
(define (auto-scale-content id target-aspect-ratio)
  (let ((dims (measure-content id)))
    (let ((w (cadr (assoc 'w dims)))

      (let ((current-ratio (/ w (max h 1))))
        (cond ((< current-ratio target-aspect-ratio) (list 'action 'stretch-width))

              (else (list 'action 'no-change)))))))
```

```motoi
(define (state-machine-patrol-combat guard alarm-trigger)
  (define (patrol waypoints idx)
    (let ((pt (list-ref waypoints idx)))

      (on-frame (lambda ()
        (if (alarm-trigger)

  (define (combat guard)

    (on-frame (lambda ()
      (when (no-targets?) (motion/halt guard) (patrol waypoints 0)))))
```

```motoi
(define (fold-left f init xs) (let loop ((xs xs) (acc init)) (if (null? xs) acc (loop (cdr xs) (f acc (car xs))))))
;; NOTE: rational literals (e.g. 1/2) deferred pending TBD-026 (decision-011 has no numerical tower).
(define (take xs n)
  (let loop ((xs xs) (n n) (acc '()))
    (if (or (zero? n) (null? xs))
        (reverse acc)
        (loop (cdr xs) (- n 1) (cons (car xs) acc)))))
(define (convergents cf-list)
  (map (lambda (n) (fold-left (lambda (acc a) (+ a (/ 1 acc))) 0 (take cf-list (+ n 1)))) (list 1 2 3)))

(convergents cf)
```

```motoi
(define (gcd-via-factors a b)
  ;; GCD is the product of common prime powers, taking min exponent
  (let ((fa (nt/prime-factorization a))
        (fb (nt/prime-factorization b)))
    (fold-left * 1
      (filter-map (lambda (pa)
                    (let ((match (assoc (car pa) fb)))
                      (if match (expt (car pa) (min (cdr pa) (cdr match))) null)))
                  fa))))
```

```motoi
(define spec 'spec)
(define my-callback 'my-callback)
(define budget 'budget)
(define with-timeout (lambda args (list "with-timeout" args)))
(define better-than? (lambda args (list "better-than?" args)))
(define (large-scale-integer-opt problem callback)
  (let loop ((remaining-time budget) (best-solution '()))
    (if (<= remaining-time 0)
      (list 'final-solution best-solution 'exhausted-time)
      (let ((candidate (ops/branch-bound (with-timeout problem remaining-time))))

          (loop (- remaining-time (time-used)) best-solution))))))
```

```motoi
(define my-graph 'my-graph)
(define (route-from-distances graph start goal)
  (let ((paths (ops/dijkstra graph start)))
    (let loop ((node goal) (path (list goal)))
      (let ((entry (assoc node paths)))
        (if (or (null? entry) (eq? node start))
            (if (eq? node start) (reverse (cons start path)) 'no-route)
            (let ((pred (cdr entry)))
              (if (pair? pred)
                  (loop (car pred) (cons (car pred) path))
                  'broken-trace)))))))
  (route-from-distances my-graph 'A 'Z)
```

```motoi
(define (fold-left f init xs)
  (let loop ((xs xs) (acc init))
    (if (null? xs) acc (loop (cdr xs) (f acc (car xs))))))
(define (iota n)
  (let loop ((i 0) (acc '()))
    (if (= i n) (reverse acc) (loop (+ i 1) (cons i acc)))))
(define (production-schedule demand unit-cost holding-cost initial)
  (let* ((periods (length demand))
         (produce (map (lambda (i) (list ':int 0 100)) (iota periods)))
         (inventory (cons initial (map (lambda (i) 0) (iota periods)))))
    (ops/mip-solve
      (map (lambda (t)
             (list 1 1 '= (list-ref demand t)))  ; balance: inventory(t-1) + produce(t) = demand(t) + inventory(t)
             (iota periods))
      (fold-left (lambda (cost t)
                   (+ cost
                      (* unit-cost (list-ref produce t))
                      (* holding-cost (list-ref inventory (+ t 1)))))
                 0 (iota periods))
      (list ':integers (list-ref produce 0) ':min? #t))))
(production-schedule (list 10 20 15) 5 2 0)
```

```motoi
(define (solve-with-fallback c A b)

    (catch-escalation
      (lambda () (ops/simplex lp))
      (lambda (tag msg)
        (cond ((eq? tag 'infeasible) (list 'status 'infeasible))
              ((eq? tag 'unbounded) (list 'status 'unbounded))
              ((eq? tag 'fuel-budget) (list 'status 'incomplete 'fuel 'exceeded))
              (else (escalate tag msg)))))))
```

```motoi
(define async-poll (lambda args (list "async-poll" args)))
(define async-ready? (lambda args (list "async-ready?" args)))
(define (async-nbody-check bodies-list time-step backend-hint)
  (let ((token (phys/nbody bodies-list (list 'use-backend backend-hint 'time-seconds time-step))))
    (define (poll-until-done token max-wait)
      (let ((result (async-poll token)))
        (if (async-ready? result)
          result
          (if (> max-wait 0)
            (begin (wait 100) (poll-until-done token (- max-wait 1)))
            '*timeout*))))
    (poll-until-done token 100)))
(async-nbody-check '((... bodies ...)) 3600 'gpu-cluster)
```

```motoi
(define (take xs n)
  (let loop ((xs xs) (n n) (acc '()))
    (if (or (zero? n) (null? xs))
        (reverse acc)
        (loop (cdr xs) (- n 1) (cons (car xs) acc)))))
(define (drop xs n)
  (let loop ((xs xs) (n n))
    (if (or (zero? n) (null? xs)) xs (loop (cdr xs) (- n 1)))))
(define (shuffle lst)
  (if (null? lst) lst
      (let* ((i (random-int (length lst)))
             (x (list-ref lst i))
             (rest (append (take lst i) (drop lst (+ i 1)))))
        (cons x (shuffle rest)))))
(shuffle '(a b c d))
```

How would an expert use `random-pick`? Show code.

```motoi
(define my-state 'my-state)
(define can-apply? (lambda args (list "can-apply?" args)))
(define move-left (lambda args (list "move-left" args)))
(define move-right (lambda args (list "move-right" args)))
(define jump (lambda args (list "jump" args)))
(define (filter pred xs)
  (let loop ((xs xs) (acc '()))
    (cond ((null? xs) (reverse acc))
          ((pred (car xs)) (loop (cdr xs) (cons (car xs) acc)))
          (else (loop (cdr xs) acc)))))
(define (sample-strategy strategies state)
  (let loop ((remaining strategies))
    (let ((pick (random-pick remaining)))
      (if (and pick (can-apply? pick state))
          (apply pick state)
          (if (null? remaining) 'fallback
              (loop (filter (lambda (s) (not (eq? s pick))) remaining)))))))
(sample-strategy '(move-left move-right jump) my-state)
```

repeatedly samples strategies until finding one applicable to the state

```motoi
(define (fold-left f init xs)
  (let loop ((xs xs) (acc init))
    (if (null? xs) acc (loop (cdr xs) (f acc (car xs))))))
;; Find the closest point in a list
(define (closest-point origin points)
  (fold-left (lambda (best pt)
              (let ((d (solve/distance (car origin) (cadr origin) (car pt) (cadr pt))))
                (if (< d (cadr best)) (list pt d) best)))
            (list (car points) 999999)
            points))
(closest-point (list 1 1) (list (list 5 5) (list 2 3) (list 10 1)))
```

```motoi
(define (fold-left f init xs)
  (let loop ((xs xs) (acc init))
    (if (null? xs) acc (loop (cdr xs) (f acc (car xs))))))
;; Centroid of a set of points: compute distance from each to the centroid
(define (centroid points)
  (let ((n (length points))
        (sum-x (apply + (map car points)))
        (sum-y (apply + (map cadr points))))
    (list (/ sum-x n) (/ sum-y n))))
(define (avg-distance-to-centroid points)
  (let ((c (centroid points)))
    (/ (fold-left (lambda (acc pt)
                    (+ acc (solve/distance (car c) (cadr c) (car pt) (cadr pt))))
                  0 points)
       (length points))))
(avg-distance-to-centroid (list (list 0 0) (list 4 0) (list 0 3)))
```

Computes centroid, then average distance to it; common in clustering and analysis.

```motoi
;; Compound percent changes: apply multiple changes and track cumulative effect
(define (compound-percent-changes values)
  (if (<= (length values) 1)
    (list 'no-change (list 0 0))
    (let loop ((acc (list 0.0 100.0)) (vals (cdr values)) (prev (car values)))
      (if (null? vals)
        acc
        (let ((pc (solve/percent-change prev (car vals))))
          (loop (list (+ (car acc) (car pc)) (* (cadr acc) (+ 1 (car pc))))
                (cdr vals)
                (car vals)))))))

```

```motoi
(define (batch-summary datasets)
  ;; Given a list of data lists, return one summary record per dataset.
  (let loop ((rest datasets) (acc '()))
    (if (null? rest)
        (reverse acc)
        (let* ((d (car rest)) (n (stat/count d)))
          (loop (cdr rest)
                (cons (if (= n 0) 'skip
                          (list ':n n ':mean (stat/mean d)))
                      acc))))))
```

```motoi
(define (empirical-cdf hist)
  ;; Given a histogram, compute the cumulative-count list — running total
  ;; of counts per bin. Useful as a discrete CDF for plotting.
  (let loop ((rest hist) (running 0) (acc '()))
    (if (null? rest)
        (reverse acc)
        (let ((c (caddr (car rest))))
          (loop (cdr rest) (+ running c) (cons (+ running c) acc))))))
```

```motoi
(define (bayes-update prior likelihood-fav likelihood-tot base-fav base-tot)
  ;; Naive Bayes numerator: prior * P(data|H) / P(data).
  ;; likelihood-* = counts under the hypothesis; base-* = counts overall.
  (let ((likelihood (stat/probability likelihood-fav likelihood-tot))
        (base      (stat/probability base-fav base-tot)))
    (if (= base 0) prior
        (/ (* prior likelihood) base))))
(bayes-update 0.3 15 30 25 100)
```

Skeleton of a Bayesian update: prior * likelihood / evidence. Returns 0.6 — likelihood 0.5, evidence 0.25, prior 0.3, so posterior = 0.3 * 0.5 / 0.25 = 0.6. Caller must renormalize across hypotheses if summing must give 1.

```motoi
(define (t-statistic sample hyp-mean)
  ;; Compute a one-sample t-statistic against a hypothesized mean.
  (let* ((n (stat/count sample))
         (mu (stat/mean sample))
         (sd (stat/sd-sample sample)))
    (if (or (< n 2) (= sd 0)) 'no-t
        (/ (- mu hyp-mean) (/ sd (sqrt n))))))
```

```motoi
  ;; Pooled variance for two samples of possibly-different size.
  (let ((na (stat/count a)) (nb (stat/count b))
        (va (stat/variance-sample a))
        (vb (stat/variance-sample b)))
    (/ (+ (* (- na 1) va) (* (- nb 1) vb))
       (- (+ na nb) 2))))
```

```motoi
(define (welch-df sa sb)
  ;; Welch's degrees of freedom for two-sample t-test with unequal variance.
  (let* ((na (stat/count sa)) (nb (stat/count sb))
         (va (stat/variance-sample sa))
         (vb (stat/variance-sample sb))
         (num (expt (+ (/ va na) (/ vb nb)) 2))
         (den (+ (/ (expt (/ va na) 2) (- na 1))
                 (/ (expt (/ vb nb) 2) (- nb 1)))))
    (if (= den 0) 0 (/ num den))))
(welch-df '(2 4 6 8 10) '(3 5 7 9 11))
```

Welch's approximate df for unequal-variance two-sample t. Returns 8 for two equal-size, equal-variance samples — matches classical df = na + nb - 2.

```motoi
  (let* ((spb (/ 60 bpm))
         (tick-ms (* spb 1000)))

      (if (< idx (length seq))
        (let ((note (list-ref seq idx)))
          (when (car note)
            (let ((res (synth/808 (cadr note) (caddr note) (cadddr note) (car (cddddr note)))))

            (lambda () (play-seq seq (+ idx 1)))))))
    (play-seq notes 0)))
```

```motoi
(define (orchestrate-motif root chord-type sections durations)
  (define (pitch-offset base semitones) base) ;; simplified pitch calc
  (define (build-chord root type)
    (cond
      ((eq? type 'maj) (list root (pitch-offset root 4) (pitch-offset root 7)))
      ((eq? type 'min) (list root (pitch-offset root 3) (pitch-offset root 7)))
      ((eq? type 'dim) (list root (pitch-offset root 3) (pitch-offset root 6)))))
  (let loop ((secs sections) (durs durations))
    (when (and (pair? secs) (pair? durs))
      (synth/chord (car secs) (build-chord root chord-type) (car durs))
      (loop (cdr secs) (cdr durs)))))
(orchestrate-motif 'C4 'maj '(strings brass woodwinds) '(2.0 1.5 1.0))
```

Compose a motif across orchestral sections; chord shape is derived from root and type, then played on each section with a specific duration.

```motoi
(define (evolve-pattern base-pat pressure-level swing)
  (define (mutate-pattern pat mutation-rate)
    (let loop ((i 0) (result ""))
      (if (>= i (string-length pat))
        result
        (let* ((char (string-ref pat i))
               (flip? (< (random) mutation-rate))
               (new-char (if flip? (if (char=? char #\0) #\1 #\0) char)))
          (loop (+ i 1) (string-append result (string new-char)))))))
  (let* ((mutated (mutate-pattern base-pat (* 0.1 pressure-level)))
         (swung (if (> swing 0.5) mutated base-pat)))  ;; simplified swing
    (synth/kit 'electronic swung 'kick)))
(evolve-pattern "1000100010001000" 0.7 0.6)
```

```motoi
(define (queue-movement-sequence movements)
  (define (load-next idx completed)
    (if (>= idx (length movements))
      completed
      (let* ((name (list-ref movements idx))
             (res (synth/load-score name))
             (status (car res)))
        (cond
          ((eq? status 'ok)
           (let ((score (cadr res)))
             (display (list 'queued name idx))
             (load-next (+ idx 1) (cons (list name score) completed))))
          ((eq? status 'pending-fetch)
           (display (list 'will-retry name)) ;;simulate retry later
           (load-next (+ idx 1) completed))
          (else
           (display (list 'failed name (cadr res)))
           (load-next (+ idx 1) completed))))))
  (reverse (load-next 0 '())))
(queue-movement-sequence '(beethoven-9-mvt-4))
```

Sequentially load multiple scores; accumulate successful loads and skip/retry failed ones.

```motoi
(define onset-listener #f)
(define (start-tap ctx)
  (let ((result (synth/onset-tap
           (lambda (event)
             (ctx-set ':onset-time (car (time/delta)) ctx)))))
    (if (eq? (car result) 'ok)
      (begin
        (set! onset-listener (cadr result))
        (next 'listening ctx))
      (escalate 'onset-failed null))))
(define (stop-tap ctx)
  (if onset-listener
    ((cadr (assoc 'unsubscribe onset-listener)) #t))
  (next 'stopped ctx))
```

```motoi
    (if (>= i (vec/dim v))
        (reverse result)
        (loop (+ i 1)
              (cons (f (vec/ref v i)) result)))))
```

```motoi
(define (dot-via-ref u v)
  (let loop ((i 0) (acc 0))
    (if (>= i (vec/dim u))
        acc
        (loop (+ i 1)
              (+ acc (* (vec/ref u i) (vec/ref v i)))))))
```

```motoi
    (let ((frames (world/tape-frames))

        (begin
          (world/tape-stop!)

        (next 'capture-until-full ctx)))
```
